corpus_size_sweep_text_heavy.finding: F-A18-1 saving > 0.85 PASS at ≥16 KB on text-heavy. The original 4 KB measurement (79%) was a corpus-size artifact, not a ceiling.

corpus_size_sweep_structured_binary.finding: Plateaus at 35-50%. 85% target NOT reachable for binary/structured classes with current algorithm.

corpus_size_sweep_repo_aggregate.finding: Mixed text-heavy aggregate at 41% v1 saving — below 85% target.

falsifier_status.F-A18-1: PARTIAL — corpus-class-dependent. PASS on text-heavy ≥16KB (hive_triad 92-95%). TRIPPED on binary/structured (n6_atlas 35-50%) and mixed JSON (nexus/anima/repo-aggregate 38-50%). Algorithmic close FAILED — order-1 model did not beat order-0 on this corpus battery; full PPM-D escape range coder deferred (LoC budget + algorithmic complexity).
falsifier_status.F-A18-2: PASS — 12+ round-trip byte-eq verifications; zero failures.
falsifier_status.F-A18-3: AOT PASS unchanged from 4b2918ec — all corpus×wire combinations < 500 ms/KB (max 27 ms/KB on v2 n6_atlas_8k). INTERP FAIL unchanged (orthogonal axis).

f_a18_1_retire_decision: NOT RETIRED — corpus-class-bounded close (text-heavy ≥16KB only). raw 71 falsifier remains active for binary/JSON corpus classes. Honest disclose per raw 91 C3.

production_gating.delta_pre_post_cycle: Pre: LATENCY-DEPLOYABLE / SAVING-NOT-DEPLOYABLE. Post: LATENCY-DEPLOYABLE / SAVING-DEPLOYABLE-CONDITIONAL (text-heavy ≥16KB). Net positive — corpus-class deploy window now characterized.

raw_137_80_pareto_contribution.estimate: A18 alone closes 30.90pp gap on hive_triad-class workloads ≥16 KB. On mixed-class workloads, A18 contributes ~10-12pp lift; reaching 80% requires stacking with A1-A4 + A12 + A15 + A18 + A9 chain (chain-amortization measurement deferred to separate ω-cycle).

raw_91_disclosures: v2 wire algorithmic strengthening attempt — order-1 byte-context model (257 freq tables × 512 entries) — MEASURED HONESTLY: did NOT beat v1 on any of the 5 standard corpora, including the 79KB extension. Root cause: per-context startup amortization needs ≥64KB on text-heavy and is unattainable on binary corpora.
F-A18-1 NOT RETIRED. Corpus-class-bounded PASS on hive_triad ≥16 KB only. The original 4-corpus snapshot was a corpus-size artifact rather than an algorithmic ceiling.
F-A18-3 AOT PASS unchanged from 4b2918ec; v2 ~2x slower than v1 on small inputs but converges on large; both <<500 ms/KB.
Round-trip byte-eq verified 12+ times across 4 original corpora × 2 wires + 5 sweep extensions × 2 wires (10 additional). All BYTE-EQ-PASS.

next_tick_proposal: Three orthogonal options for full corpus-class deploy: (a) full PPM-D escape range coder with sparse-context handling (≫300 LoC), OR (b) cross-file dictionary fed via A19 layer (already shipped — pair A18×A19 chain measurement), OR (c) corpus-specific pre-trained dictionary (raw 137 axis 80% Pareto extension). Option (b) recommended next — A19 already lands; chain-amortization ω-cycle is the highest-leverage step.

live_fire_v3.wire: v3 (lz-ppm-v3, b64 body, hashed order-3 byte-context — 4096 buckets × 512)
live_fire_v3.raw_91_honest_c3: v3 hashed order-3 model is WORSE than v1 AND v2 on all 4 measured corpus classes. Root cause: 4096-bucket warm-up amortization needs ≈30K tokens-per-bucket convergence; 79KB inputs yield ≈30K total tokens = ≈7 tokens per bucket — insufficient adaptive signal. Per-class CEILING lift FAILED. v3 is BUILT, byte-eq verified, and CORRECTLY rejected by a18_encode min-of-4 try-and-revert (raw 142 D2).

raw_137_80_pareto_verdict_v3.verdict: ❌ 80% TARGET NOT REACHED — A18 v3 hashed order-3 dispatcher does not contribute saving% lift; aggregate is byte-identical to v2-dispatcher baseline because v3 is rejected on every measured corpus by raw 142 D2 try-revert.
raw_137_80_pareto_verdict_v3.structural_finding: a38cd46d A25 verdict reconfirmed AND extended: not only is the dispatcher-only path structurally bounded, but algorithmic order-N+1 lift via hashed-bucket adaptive coding is ALSO blocked by warm-up amortization at ≤79KB input sizes. 80% close path requires either (a) full sparse PPM-D with escape mechanism (≫600 LoC, unbounded memory), OR (b) cross-file dictionary chain (A18×A19 — recommended), OR (c) per-class pretrained dictionary.
